fields.label: Mode
fields.description: Choose **Hosted Datadog Metrics**, if you want to connect using a Datadog proxy through [Hosted Datadog Metrics](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/data-configuration/metrics/metrics-datadog/). Otherwise choose **Default** to directly connect to DataDog API endpoints.

fields.label: API URL / Region
fields.description: A URL to the Datadog API (e.g.: https://api.datadoghq.com)

overrides.description: A URL to the Hosted metrics API (e.g.: https://dd-prod-10-prod-us-central-0.grafana.net/datadog). [Click here](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/data-configuration/metrics/metrics-datadog/) to learn more.

fields.label: API key
fields.description: An API key is unique to your organization. [Learn more](https://grafana.com/docs/plugins/grafana-datadog-datasource/latest/#get-an-api-key-and-application-key-from-datadog).

fields.label: App key
fields.description: An application key is used with the API key to give access to the Datadog API. By default, application keys have the permissions of the user who created them. [Learn more](https://grafana.com/docs/plugins/grafana-datadog-datasource/latest/#get-api-key-and-application-key-from-datadog). You can also customize the scope of the application key in the [Datadog docs](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/scopes/).

fields.label: User
fields.description: Your username is your Grafana Cloud Prometheus username. This can be found in the Prometheus details in your cloud portal.

fields.label: Password
fields.description: Your password is your Grafana Cloud API Key with read permissions. This can be found in the Prometheus details in your cloud portal.

fields.label: Show API rate limits
fields.description: Show Datadog API limits for each queried endpoint. To view the API rate limits, go to the **Query Inspector**, select **JSON**, and set **select source** to **DataFrame structure**.

fields.label: Enable API rate limit threshold
fields.description: Enable rate limit. Datadog query will stop once it reaches entered threshold.

fields.label: API rate limit threshold %
fields.description: Enter percentage of threshold. (If the API hit the % of rate limit, plugin will block subsequent requests till next reset)

fields.label: Disable data links
fields.description: Data links take users directly to the relevant location in the Datadog app when they interact with panels.

fields.label: Response Size
fields.description: Set maximum number of items to retrieve in a single API request (default is 100).

relationships.description: Connection/auth mode discriminator. jsonData.pluginMode ('default' or 'hosted-metrics') is the stored value; root.basicAuth is set true only in hosted-metrics mode. The editor's Mode selector (virtual_mode) writes both, and reads back a legacy datasource with root.basicAuth=true but no pluginMode as hosted-metrics (ConfigEditor.tsx:527-535; pkg/models/settings.go:84-92).

Default-mode credentials: the Datadog API key (DD-API-KEY header) and Application key (DD-APPLICATION-KEY header) are used together. Both are required when pluginMode != 'hosted-metrics' (pkg/datadog/health_diagnostics.go:82-89; pkg/datadog/client/client_v1.go:97-99).

Hosted-metrics credentials: the Grafana Cloud basic-auth username (root.basicAuthUser) and password (secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword), injected as URL userinfo against the hosted-metrics proxy (pkg/datadog/client/client_v1.go:85-87). Both are required when pluginMode == 'hosted-metrics' (pkg/datadog/health_diagnostics.go:90-100).

The API rate limit threshold input is only rendered when jsonData.rateLimitEnabled is true (ConfigEditor.tsx:406-425); when enabled but left at 0 the backend coerces it to 100 (pkg/models/settings.go:63-65).

instructions.msg: Two connection/authentication modes are selected by jsonData.pluginMode: 'default' (the default) connects directly to the Datadog API and authenticates with an API key + Application key stored in secureJsonData (apiKey, appKey); 'hosted-metrics' connects through a Grafana Cloud Datadog proxy and authenticates with datasource root basic auth (root.basicAuth=true, root.basicAuthUser, secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword). The editor's Mode radio drives everything: it sets jsonData.pluginMode and toggles root.basicAuth together, and only the selected mode's credential fields are shown.

To configure Default (direct API) mode, set jsonData.pluginMode to 'default', jsonData.url to a Datadog API URL (region), and put both secrets in secureJsonData. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"pluginMode":"default","url":"https://api.datadoghq.com"},"secureJsonData":{"apiKey":"<your-datadog-api-key>","appKey":"<your-application-key>"}}. Both apiKey and appKey are mandatory in this mode — the backend health check returns 'Enter an API key.' / 'Enter an App key.' otherwise (pkg/datadog/health_diagnostics.go:82-89). They are sent as the DD-API-KEY and DD-APPLICATION-KEY request headers.

To configure Hosted Datadog Metrics mode, set jsonData.pluginMode to 'hosted-metrics', set root.basicAuth to true, jsonData.url to the Grafana Cloud hosted-metrics proxy URL, root.basicAuthUser to your Grafana Cloud Prometheus username, and secureJsonData.basicAuthPassword to your Grafana Cloud API key. Minimal payload: {"basicAuth":true,"basicAuthUser":"<your-grafana-cloud-username>","jsonData":{"pluginMode":"hosted-metrics","url":"https://dd-prod-10-prod-us-central-0.grafana.net/datadog"},"secureJsonData":{"basicAuthPassword":"<your-grafana-cloud-api-key>"}}. The URL must NOT be the default https://api.datadoghq.com, and both the username and password are required (pkg/datadog/health_diagnostics.go:90-100).

Legacy interpretation: datasources created by the 1.x plugin stored api_key and app_key as plaintext under jsonData; on load the editor and backend migrate them into secureJsonData.apiKey / secureJsonData.appKey and stop reading the jsonData copies (ConfigEditor.tsx:41-68; pkg/models/settings.go:52-58). Also, a datasource with root.basicAuth=true but no jsonData.pluginMode is treated as 'hosted-metrics' (pkg/models/settings.go:84-92) — do not assume a missing pluginMode means Default mode when basic auth is enabled.

secureJsonData values (apiKey, appKey, basicAuthPassword) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.apiKey == true) instead of secureJsonData.

jsonData.url is required in every mode and its meaning depends on pluginMode: in Default mode it is the regional Datadog API base URL — one of https://api.datadoghq.com (US1), https://api.us3.datadoghq.com (US3), https://api.us5.datadoghq.com (US5), https://api.datadoghq.eu (EU), https://api.ddog-gov.com (US1-FED), or a custom value; in Hosted Datadog Metrics mode it is the Grafana Cloud proxy URL. The backend joins '/api/v1' (and '/api/v2') onto this URL (pkg/datadog/client/client_v1.go:88,118), so provide the scheme+host with no trailing path. An empty url fails the health check with 'Enter a URL.' (pkg/datadog/health_diagnostics.go:101-103).
